sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tom U. @c_APT_ure (collection), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of the bcdedit command to tamper with the boot configuration data. This technique is often times used by malware or attackers as a destructive way before launching ransomware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Usage of Renamed Sysinternals Tools - RegistrySet
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects non-sysinternals tools setting the "accepteula" key which normally is set on sysinternals tool execution

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Process Explorer Driver Creation By Non-Sysinternals Binary
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects creation of the Process Explorer drivers by processes other than Process Explorer (procexp) itself.
Hack tools or malware may use the Process Explorer driver to elevate privileges, drops it to disk for a few moments, runs a service using that driver and removes it afterwards.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

values.Description: Command Line Interface for Microsoft® Volume Shadow Copy Service

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects loading of known vulnerable drivers via their hash.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Schedule Task Creation From Env Variable Or Potentially Suspicious Path Via Schtasks.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects Schtask creations that point to a suspicious folder or an environment variable often used by malware

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Via Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects registry value settings that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.

Detects installation of NalDrv or PROCEXP152 services via registry-keys to non-system32 folders.
Both services are used in the tool Ghost-In-The-Logs (https://github.com/bats3c/Ghost-In-The-Logs), which uses KDU (https://github.com/hfiref0x/KDU)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the execution of "reg.exe" to alter registry keys that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\system32\cmd.exe" /C reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v Wallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Users\george\AppData\Roaming\fCP0JZYY.bmp" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v WallpaperStyle /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v TileWallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f

"C:\Windows\system32\cmd.exe" /C reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v Wallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Users\george\AppData\Roaming\fCP0JZYY.bmp" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v WallpaperStyle /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f & reg add "HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop" /v TileWallpaper /t REG_SZ /d "0" /f

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects unusual processes accessing desktop.ini, which can be leveraged to alter how Explorer displays a folder's content (i.e. renaming files) without changing them on disk.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.
Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: UPX dump on OEP (original entry point)
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: kevoreilly

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: YARA rule to detect if a sqllang.dll version is targeted by skip-2.0. Each byte pattern corresponds to a function hooked by skip-2.0. If $1_0 or $1_1 match, it is probably targeted as it corresponds to the hook responsible for bypassing the authentication.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Mathieu Tartare <mathieu.tartare@eset.com>

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (console) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET [139,445] ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER SMBv1 protocol detection attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"|FF|SMB|72 00 00 00 00|"; content:"|02|PC NETWORK PROGRAM 1.0",fast_pattern,nocase; service:netbios-ssn; reference:url,github.com/rapid7/metasploit-framework/blob/master/modules/auxiliary/scanner/smb/smb1.rb; classtype:policy-violation; gid:1; sid:44484; rev:6; )

alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET [139,445] ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER SMBv1 protocol detection attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"|FF|SMB|72 00 00 00 00|"; content:"|02|LM1.2X002",fast_pattern,nocase; service:netbios-ssn; reference:url,github.com/rapid7/metasploit-framework/blob/master/modules/auxiliary/scanner/smb/smb1.rb; classtype:policy-violation; sid:44487; rev:4; )

alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET [139,445] ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER SMBv1 protocol detection attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"|FF|SMB|72 00 00 00 00|"; content:"|02|LANMAN2.1",fast_pattern,nocase; service:netbios-ssn; reference:url,github.com/rapid7/metasploit-framework/blob/master/modules/auxiliary/scanner/smb/smb1.rb; classtype:policy-violation; sid:44488; rev:4; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: SURICATA Applayer Detect protocol only one direction
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert ip any any -> any any (msg:"SURICATA Applayer Detect protocol only one direction"; flow:established; app-layer-event:applayer_detect_protocol_only_one_direction; flowint:applayer.anomaly.count,+,1; classtype:protocol-command-decode; sid:2260002; rev:1;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: NETBIOS SMB srvsvc named pipe creation attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp any any -> $HOME_NET 445 ( msg:"NETBIOS SMB srvsvc named pipe creation attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"|FE|SMB|40 00|",depth 6,offset 4; content:"|05 00|",within 2,distance 6; content:"s|00|r|00|v|00|s|00|v|00|c|00|",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop; service:netbios-ssn; classtype:misc-activity; sid:38320; rev:2; )
